frameworks.name: locus coeruleus-noradrenaline pupil-linked arousal
frameworks.one_line: Phasic LC-NA activity, indexed by pupil dilation, modulates encoding-related plasticity.

frameworks.name: noradrenergic synaptic plasticity account of memory
frameworks.one_line: NA release during encoding is hypothesized to consolidate memory traces.

core_question: Do task-irrelevant sounds that boost phasic pupil-linked arousal also enhance memory formation?
key_assumptions: Pupil dilation indexes phasic LC-NA arousal
Encoding-time arousal causally supports memory consolidation
Task-irrelevant sounds reliably drive pupil-linked arousal
manipulations: Task-irrelevant white-noise sounds present vs absent
Sound timing relative to memoranda (before, during, after)
Memory test type (recognition vs free recall, next day)
key_findings: Trial-by-trial encoding pupil dilations (no sound) predicted later memory success
Task-irrelevant white-noise sounds robustly dilated the pupil
Sound-evoked pupil dilations did not improve recognition or free recall for words or images
interpretations: Exogenous, sound-driven pupil-linked arousal is dissociable from endogenous encoding arousal
Phasic LC-NA arousal alone is not sufficient to enhance memory formation
